candidates.row_label: Depreciation & Amortization in Cost of Revenues
candidates.why: Explicitly labelled as COGS-related D&A, but it appears to be a sub-component within the cost-of-revenues bucket rather than a separate same-level expense outside the already selected parent row 'Cost of revenues/COGS'. Adding it separately would risk double counting.

This is a broader operating D&A line and not clearly restricted to COGS. Given the presence of a specific '...in Cost of Revenues' label, the generic line is not the best COGS-specific add-back choice.

variables.notes: The selected COGS reference bucket is the parent row 'Cost of revenues/COGS'. The visible COGS-specific D&A label appears to sit within that bucket, so no separate COGS_DA row should be added.

candidates.why: This is the only populated operating D&A row above EBIT, and it appears as a separate same-level operating line relative to the selected XSGA component rows. However, it is generic and may include COGS-related D&A as well, so assigning it wholly to XSGA would be risky.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation & Amortization in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: This is the most directly relevant SG&A-specific label, but it is presented like a sub-component/detail line and is blank in the excerpt. It does not clearly support a separate add-back relative to the already selected XSGA component bucket.
Also directly SG&A-specific, but it appears as a component/detail line and is blank in the excerpt. No clear separate populated SG&A D&A row is visible to add.

R&D D&A should not be added here because 'Production and Development Expenses' is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, so adding an explicit R&D D&A row would risk distorting the intended XSGA minus XRD logic. This row is also blank.

variables.notes: No clear populated SG&A-only D&A row is visible above EBIT. The generic row 'Depreciation and Amortization' may be separate operating D&A, but it is not clearly attributable only to XSGA and may include COGS. To avoid double counting and misallocation, no XSGA_DA row is selected, but manual review is warranted.
